title: string, array and vector

canonicalExplanation.summary: Sequence containers preserve element order but offer different insertion, erasure, random-access, contiguity, and iterator-invalidation guarantees.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A vector stores scores for fast indexed traversal and reserves known capacity before retaining references during append operations. Your goal is to select and implement with string, array or vector for a stated ownership and size requirement. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Loops and range iteration; Copy and assignment semantics. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: Sequence containers preserve element order but offer different insertion, erasure, random-access, contiguity, and iterator-invalidation guarantees. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Vector reallocation invalidates references, deque layout is not one contiguous array, list sacrifices locality, and complexity guarantees do not include every constant cost. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: All standard sequence containers support the same iterator stability and random-access operations.

examples.title: string, array and vector: boundary case
examples.situation: A list is rejected for small numeric iteration because pointer overhead and poor locality outweigh cheap middle insertion not actually needed.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A list is rejected for small numeric iteration because pointer overhead and poor locality outweigh cheap middle insertion not actually needed.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Vector reallocation invalidates references, deque layout is not one contiguous array, list sacrifices locality, and complexity guarantees do not include every constant cost.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: All standard sequence containers support the same iterator stability and random-access operations.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model string, array and vector: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
